sections.label: Section 1440
sections.original: στέλλῃ πρὸς οἴκους ἢ μένων εὐδαιμονῇς .
sections.translation: set out for home or remain here in happiness . Exit Theoklymenos .

sections.label: Section 1441
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ , πατήρ τε καὶ σοφὸς κλῄζῃ θεός ,
sections.translation: O Zeus , called father and god of wisdom , look on us and alter our woes ! As we drag our fortunes to the rocky hill , make haste to join with us ; if you touch us with your finger-tip only ,

sections.label: Section 1445
sections.original: ἥξομεν ἵνʼ ἐλθεῖν βουλόμεσθα τῆς τύχης .
sections.translation: we shall reach our longed-for goal . There has been enough distress in what we have suffered before . I have invoked you , gods , with many names , good and painful ; I am not bound to be unfortunate forever , but to advance in a straight course . If you grant me one favor ,

sections.label: Section 1450
sections.original: δόντες τὸ λοιπὸν εὐτυχῆ με θήσετε .
sections.translation: you will make me fortunate hereafter . Exeunt Menelaos and Helen .

sections.label: Section 1.1451
sections.original: Φοίνισσα Σιδωνιὰς ὦ
sections.translation: O swift Phoenician ship of Sidon , dear to the surging waves , mother of the oar , leader of the lovely dancing

sections.label: Section 1.1455
sections.original: δελφίνων , ὅταν αὔραις
sections.translation: of dolphins , when the sea is clear of breezes and Ocean ’ s gray-green daughter , spirit of calm , says these words : Spread your sails

sections.label: Section 1.1460
sections.original: ραις λιπόντες εἰναλίαις ,
sections.translation: to the sea-breezes , as you go on your way , grasp your oars of pine , oh ! sailors , sailors , speeding Helen on her way to the shore with good harbor , where once Perseus lived . .

sections.label: Section 1.1465
sections.original: ἦ που κόρας ἂν ποταμοῦ
sections.translation: Perhaps you may find the daughters of Leukippos beside the swell of the river or before the temple of Pallas , when at last you join in the dances or the revels of Hyakinthos

sections.label: Section 1.1470
sections.original: νύχιον ἐς εὐφροσύναν ,
sections.translation: in night-long joy — Hyakinthos , whom Phoebus killed with the round discus , contesting for the farthest throw — a day of the sacrifice of oxen in the Lakonian land ;

sections.label: Section 1.1475
sections.original: ὁ Διὸς δʼ εἶπε σέβειν γόνος ·
sections.translation: the son of Zeus declared that his race would be honored ; and you may find the girl whom you left at home , Hermione , for as yet no torch has lit the way to her marriage .

sections.label: Section 2.1478
sections.original: διʼ ἀέρος εἴθε ποτανοὶ
sections.translation: Oh , that we had wings to cleave the air , where the birds of Libya go in their ranks ,

sections.label: Section 2.1480
sections.original: οἰωνοὶ στοχάδες
sections.translation: leaving the winter rain , obedient to the piping of their veteran leader , who raises his exultant cry

sections.label: Section 2.1485
sections.original: ποιμένος , ὃς ἄβροχα πεδία καρποφόρα τε γᾶς
sections.translation: as he wings his way over unmoistened and crop-bearing plains of the earth . O you winged long-necked comrades of the racing clouds , go on beneath the Pleiades in their central station

sections.label: Section 2.1490
sections.original: ὠρίωνά τʼ ἐννύχιον ·
sections.translation: and Orion of the night ; deliver the message , as you settle on Eurotas ’ banks , that Menelaos has sacked the city of Dardanos , and will come home .

sections.label: Section 2.1495
sections.original: μόλοιτέ ποθʼ ἵππιον οἶμον
sections.translation: May you come at last , speeding over your horses ’ path through the sky , sons of Tyndareus , under the whirling of the radiant stars ; you who dwell in heaven , Helen ’ s rescuers ,

sections.label: Section 2.1500
sections.original: σωτῆρες τᾶς Ἑλένας ,
sections.translation: go over the gray-green swell and the dark gray surge of sea-waves , sending the sailors

sections.label: Section 2.1505
sections.original: πέμποντες Διόθεν πνοάς ·
sections.translation: favoring breezes from Zeus ; and cast away from your sister her ill-fame from marriage with a barbarian , the punishment she received from the contest on Ida ;

sections.label: Section 2.1510
sections.original: οὐκ ἐλθοῦσά ποτʼ Ἰλίου
sections.translation: but she never went to the land of Ilion , to the towers of Phoebus .

sections.label: Section 1512
sections.original: ἄναξ , τὰ κάκιστʼ ἐν δόμοις εὑρήκαμεν ·
sections.translation: ( entering hurriedly .) O king , at last have I found thee in the palace ; for new . . . troubles are you soon to hear from me .

sections.label: Section 1514
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν ;
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 1514b
sections.original: ἄλλης ἐκπόνει μνηστεύματα
sections.translation: Go to work on the courtship of another

sections.label: Section 1515
sections.original: γυναικός · Ἑλένη γὰρ βέβηκʼ ἔξω χθονός .
sections.translation: woman ; for Helen has left the country .

sections.label: Section 1516
sections.original: πτεροῖσιν ἀρθεῖσʼ ἢ πεδοστιβεῖ ποδί ;
sections.translation: Carried up on wings , or treading on the earth ?

sections.label: Section 1517
sections.original: Μενέλαος αὐτὴν ἐκπεπόρθμευται χθονός ,
sections.translation: Menelaos has taken her off as plunder , out of the land ; he was the one that came with the news of his own death .

sections.label: Section 1519
sections.original: ὦ δεινὰ λέξας · τίς δέ νιν ναυκληρία
sections.translation: O dreadful story ! What ship carried her

sections.label: Section 1520
sections.original: ἐκ τῆσδʼ ἀπῆρε χθονός ; ἄπιστα γὰρ λέγεις .
sections.translation: off from this land ? Your story is unbelievable .

sections.label: Section 1521
sections.original: ἥν γε ξένῳ δίδως σύ · τούς τε σοὺς ἔχων
sections.translation: The very one that you gave to the stranger ; and he has gone with your sailors , so that you may know everything in brief .

sections.label: Section 1523
sections.original: πῶς ; εἰδέναι πρόθυμος · οὐ γὰρ ἐλπίδων
sections.translation: How ? I am eager to know ; for I never expected that a single hand could excel over

sections.label: Section 1525
sections.original: τοσούσδε ναύτας , ὧν ἀπεστάλης μέτα .
sections.translation: so many sailors , with whom you were sent .

sections.label: Section 1526
sections.original: ἐπεὶ λιποῦσα τούσδε βασιλείους δόμους
sections.translation: When the daughter of Zeus had left this royal house and started for the sea , delicately picking her way , she most cleverly began to mourn her husband , though he was close at hand and not dead .

sections.label: Section 1530
sections.original: ὡς δʼ ἤλθομεν σῶν περίβολον νεωρίων ,
sections.translation: When we reached the enclosure of your dockyards , we began to launch the Sidonian ship on her first voyage , with her fifty benches and full measure of rowers . Task gave way to task : one set up the mast , another set up the oars

sections.label: Section 1535
sections.original: ταρσόν τε χειρί , λευκά θʼ ἱστίʼ εἰς ἓν ἦν
sections.translation: . . . and the rudders were lowered by their cross-bars . And during this labor , men of Hellas who had been fellow-voyagers with Menelaos were watching us , it seems , and they drew near to the beach , clad in the rags of shipwrecked men ,

sections.label: Section 1540
sections.original: πέπλοισιν , εὐειδεῖς μέν , αὐχμηροὶ δʼ ὁρᾶν .
sections.translation: handsome , but rough to look upon . And the son of Atreus , when he saw them approach , spoke to them , craftily introducing the reason for his mourning : Unhappy sailors , how have you arrived ? From the wreckage of what Achaean ship ?

sections.label: Section 1545
sections.original: ἆρʼ Ἀτρέως παῖδʼ ὀλόμενον συνθάπτετε ,
sections.translation: Are you here to help bury the dead son of Atreus , whose missing body this lady , daughter of Tyndareus , is honoring with an empty grave ? They wept in a feigned manner , and went to the ship , carrying aboard the offerings to be thrown into the sea for Menelaos . We were suspicious at this ,

sections.label: Section 1550
sections.original: λόγος τʼ ἐν ἀλλήλοισι , τῶν ἐπεσβατῶν
sections.translation: and said to each other that there would be a crowd of those additional passengers ; but still we remained silent , out of respect for your orders ; for by bidding the stranger command the vessel , you threw everything into confusion .

sections.label: Section 1554
sections.original: καὶ τἄλλα μὲν δὴ ῥᾳδίως ἔσω νεὼς
sections.translation: Well , we easily put the other victims on the ship , for they were light ;

sections.label: Section 1555
sections.original: ἐθέμεθα κουφίζοντα · ταύρειος δὲ ποὺς
sections.translation: but the bull did not want to go forward along the plank , but kept bellowing loudly , rolling his eyes around ; and , arching his back and peering along his horns , he prevented us from touching him . But Helen ’ s husband

sections.label: Section 1560
sections.original: ἐκάλεσεν · ὦ πέρσαντες Ἰλίου πόλιν ,
sections.translation: called out : O you who sacked the town of Ilion , come pick up this bull on young shoulders , as is the way in Hellas , and cast him into the prow . . . the sacrifice to the dead man .

sections.label: Section 1565
sections.original: οἳ δʼ ἐς κέλευσμʼ ἐλθόντες ἐξανήρπασαν
sections.translation: Then they came at his summons , and caught up the bull and carried him on to the deck . And Menelaos stroked the horse on neck and brow , coaxing it to go aboard .

sections.label: Section 1569
sections.original: τέλος δʼ , ἐπειδὴ ναῦς τὰ πάντʼ ἐδέξατο ,
sections.translation: Finally , when the ship was fully loaded ,

sections.label: Section 1570
sections.original: πλήσασα κλιμακτῆρας εὐσφύρῳ ποδί
sections.translation: Helen climbed up the ladder with elegant step , and took her seat in the middle of the rowers ’ benches , and he was near by , Menelaos who was called dead . The rest , equally divided on the right and left sides of the ship , sat down , each beside his man , with swords concealed beneath their cloaks ,

sections.label: Section 1575
sections.original: λαθραῖʼ ἔχοντες , ῥόθιά τʼ ἐξεπίμπλατο
sections.translation: and the waves were filled with shouting as we heard the voice of the boatswain .

sections.label: Section 1577
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δὲ γαίας ἦμεν οὔτʼ ἄγαν πρόσω
sections.translation: Now when we had put out from land , neither very far nor very near , the helmsman asked , Shall we sail yet further , stranger , or is this far enough ?

sections.label: Section 1580
sections.original: πλεύσωμεν ; ἀρχαὶ γὰρ νεὼς μέλουσι σοί .
sections.translation: For the command of this ship is yours . And he answered , Far enough for me . Holding a sword in his right hand , he stepped into the prow ; and , standing over the bull to slay it , with no mention of any dead man , he cut its throat and prayed : O Poseidon of the sea ,

sections.label: Section 1585
sections.original: πόντιε Πόσειδον Νηρέως θʼ ἁγναὶ κόραι ,
sections.translation: who lives in the deep , and you holy daughters of Nereus , bring me and my wife safe and sound from here to Nauplia ’ s shore ! Streams of blood , a good omen for the stranger , darted into the waves . And someone said , There is treachery in this voyage ;

sections.label: Section 1590
sections.original: πάλιν πλέωμεν · δεξιὰν κέλευε σύ ,
sections.translation: let us sail back again ! You , give an order for the right oar , you , turn your rudder . But the son of Atreus , standing where he slew the bull , cried out to his comrades , Why do you , the pick of Hellas , delay to slaughter and kill the barbarians

sections.label: Section 1595
sections.original: ῥίπτειν ἐς οἶδμα ; ναυβάταις δὲ τοῖσι σοῖς
sections.translation: and hurl them from the ship into the waves ? And the boatswain cried the opposite command to your rowers : Some of you catch up planks at the end , break up the benches , or snatch the oars from the locks , and make the heads of these foreign enemies bloody ! .

sections.label: Section 1600
sections.original: ὀρθοὶ δʼ ἀνῇξαν πάντες , οἳ μὲν ἐν χεροῖν
sections.translation: They all leapt upright , some with oars in their hands , others with swords ; and the ship ran with blood . Helen cheered them on from the stern : Where is the fame you won in Troy ? Show it against the barbarians ! In their eagerness , some would

sections.label: Section 1605
sections.original: ἔπιπτον , οἳ δʼ ὠρθοῦντο , τοὺς δὲ κειμένους
sections.translation: fall , some stood upright , you would have seen others lying dead . But Menelaos , in full armor , wherever he spied that his comrades were suffering , would go there , sword in hand ; and so we dived

sections.label: Section 1610
sections.original: σῶν ναυβατῶν ἐρετμά . ἐπʼ οἰάκων δὲ βὰς
sections.translation: from the ship , and he cleared the benches of your rowers . Then going to the helmsman he told him to sail a straight course to Hellas . So they set up the mast , and favoring breezes blew .

sections.label: Section 1613
sections.original: βεβᾶσι δʼ ἐκ γῆς . διαφυγὼν δʼ ἐγὼ φόνον
sections.translation: They are gone from here . But I escaped death and let myself down by the anchor into the sea ;

sections.label: Section 1615
sections.original: ἤδη δὲ κάμνονθʼ ὁρμιατόνων μέ τις
sections.translation: and just as I was worn out , some fisherman took me up , and put me out on land , to bring you this report . Nothing is more useful to mankind than a prudent distrust .

sections.label: Section 1619
sections.original: οὐκ ἄν ποτʼ ηὔχουν οὔτε σʼ οὔθʼ ἡμᾶς λαθεῖν
sections.translation: I never would have believed that Menelaos could have eluded both us

sections.label: Section 1620
sections.original: Μενέλαον , ὦναξ , ὡς ἐλάνθανεν παρών .
sections.translation: and you , O king , the way he did on his arrival .

sections.label: Section 1621
sections.original: ὦ γυναικείαις τέχναισιν αἱρεθεὶς ἐγὼ τάλας ·
sections.translation: Caught by a woman ’ s tricks , unhappy that I am ! My bride has escaped me . If the ship could have been pursued and overtaken , I would have made an effort to catch the strangers at once ; but now I will avenge myself upon my treacherous sister ,

sections.label: Section 1625
sections.original: ἥτις ἐν δόμοις ὁρῶσα Μενέλεων οὐκ εἶπέ μοι .
sections.translation: for she saw Menelaos in my house and did not tell me . Therefore she will never deceive another man by her oracles .

sections.label: Section 1627
sections.original: οὗτος ὤ , ποῖ σὸν πόδʼ αἴρεις , δέσποτʼ , ἐς ποῖον φόνον ;
sections.translation: You , there ! Where are you rushing off to , my lord ? to what bloody deed ?

sections.label: Section 1628
sections.original: οἷπερ ἡ δίκη κελεύει με · ἀλλʼ ἀφίστασʼ ἐκποδών .
sections.translation: Where justice calls me . Get out of my way !

sections.label: Section 1629
sections.original: οὐκ ἀφήσομαι πέπλων σῶν · μεγάλα γὰρ σπεύδεις κακά .
sections.translation: I will not let go of your robe , for you are striving after great wickedness .

sections.label: Section 1630
sections.original: ἀλλὰ δεσποτῶν κρατήσεις δοῦλος ὤν ;
sections.translation: Will you rule over your master , although you are a slave ?
